tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of significant renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria, low urine specific gravity), mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride), and hypoglycemia (low glucose, low HbA1c). Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) suggest possible underlying renal or hepatic pathology, but no clear evidence of infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for causes of hypoglycemia and renal impairment.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
